channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: GloRilla conquers self-doubt on her path to becoming one of hip-hop's most promising voices
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — GloRilla burst onto the hip-hop scene, lighting up the rap world a couple years ago with breakout tracks like “ Tomorrow ” and “ F.N.F. (Let’s Go) ” — even snagging a Grammy nomination. But as her name skyrocketed, so did the pressure.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Anti-Israel demonstrators set up camp outside Jewish congressman’s home
item.description: Anti-Israeli protesters have set up an encampment outside the home of Rep. Greg Landsman (D-Ohio), who has decried their activity as harassment. Landsman, who is Jewish, shared a photo online of a group of demonstrators outside his home. The protests are taking place a year after the Oct.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Missouri woman finds trunk full of stolen campaign signs with help of Apple AirTag
item.description: "I expected to find the AirTag, but not 59 signs. It was kind of like finding a dead body," the Missouri woman said.
item.dc:creator: Parker Padgett, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court ruled Tuesday that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rep. Brandon Williams and challenger John Mannion square off in NY-22 debate
item.description: Debate between candidates in the 22nd Congressional District. Featuring incumbent Brandon Williams and challenger NY State Senator John Mannion.
item.dc:creator: Dave Longley, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio man charged with bringing massive 'Trump' sign to Capitol for rioters to use as a weapon
item.description: An Ohio man was arrested Tuesday on charges that he brought a massive “Trump” sign to the U.S. Capitol and joined other rioters in using it as a weapon against police officers during a mob attack.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge tosses RNC challenge to Michigan voter roll maintenance
item.description: U.S. District Judge Jane Beckering ruled that neither the RNC nor two individual voters who joined the suit had legal standing to bring their case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Trump airs list of familiar grievances as he and Harris seek Latino votes
item.description: MIAMI, Florida (AP) — Donald Trump on Tuesday described Democratic opponent Kamala Harris as “lazy," railed against green energy and talked about “extreme" presidential power during a campaign event promoted as a business economic roundtable aimed at Latino voters.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, JILL COLVIN and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Social Security funds under Trump could run out in 6 years: Analysis
item.description: In an analysis published Monday, the fiscal watchdog weighed the impact several Trump proposals — including his plan to end taxation of Social Security benefits
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: MLB wants the Tampa Bay Rays to play in the area if hurricane-damaged Tropicana Field is unfit
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — Major League Baseball hopes to find the Tampa Bay Rays a temporary home near their fanbase at least for the start of the 2025 season if the damage to Tropicana Field from Hurricane Milton cannot be repaired in time. The translucent fiberglass dome at the ballpark in St.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury finds ex-Bosnian prison camp guard guilty for lying about refugee status
item.description: A former guard at Celebici prison, which was notorious for being harsh toward inmates, was convicted of lying about his involvement at the prison when he gained U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How third-party candidates could make a difference in swing states
item.description: Third-party candidates will be on the ballot in all of the top battleground states, drawing increased scrutiny over how they could influence the outcome of the presidential election in two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Insurance company refuses to pay 82-year-old Las Vegas woman's claim after her home burned down
item.description: Two weeks before the fire, the insurance company canceled the woman's policy, accusing her of running a motorcycle repair shop out of her home — something the 82-year-old woman denies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, Fox 5

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, WGN 9

item.title: A true homecoming for one Virginia family made the Navy town proud
item.description: From the sideline Lt. Cmdr. Tatasciore of the USS New Hampshire, aka Dad, sailed in just as his children were on the 50-yard line.
item.dc:creator: Regina Mobley, NewsNation

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob during an Oval Office meeting for his key role in a recent seven-nation prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Foster kids in downtown Memphis housing units out of control: Neighbors
item.description: This comes a year after the Tennessee Department of Children’s Services said children in state care were sleeping on floors.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Federal lawsuit filed against use of Flock cameras in Norfolk
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (WAVY) — A federal lawsuit has been filed on behalf of two area residents who are challenging the legality of using Flock cameras in the city of Norfolk.
item.dc:creator: Jimmy LaRoue, NewsNation

item.title: Selena Quintanilla, Missy Elliot, Steven Spielberg and more honored by Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday honored acclaimed filmmakers, singers, writers and others who have made their mark on American culture, awarding the prestigious National Medals of Arts and National Humanities Medals to 39 recipients.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judges reject GOP overseas ballot challenges in Michigan, North Carolina
item.description: State judges in Michigan and North Carolina on Monday rejected the Republican National Committee’s (RNC) separate challenges to certain overseas ballots in the key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: McDonald's says it doesn't endorse political candidates after Trump visit
item.description: McDonald's is emphasizing its political neutrality after former President Donald Trump spent some time behind the counter at one of its Pennsylvania locations.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: White House proposes insurers cover cost of over-the-counter birth control
item.description: The White House on Monday announced a proposal that would require insurers to cover the cost of forms of contraception like over-the-counter birth control pills and condoms under the Affordable Care Act.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct Cher, Foreigner, Mary J. Blige, Ozzy and more
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Hollywood stars Julia Roberts and Zendaya bookended Saturday’s inductions into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, giving a little stardust to an eclectic lineup that included pop icon Cher, heavy metal’s Ozzy Osbourne, hip-hop soul queen Mary J.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is now offering sleeveless garments for members in hot and humid climates, according to a Church spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, KRON4

item.title: One of the last Navajo Code Talkers from World War II dies at 107
item.description: John Kinsel Sr., one of the last remaining Navajo Code Talkers who transmitted messages during World War II based on the tribe's native language, has died. He was 107.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Zealand wins cricket's Women’s T20 World Cup for 1st time with 32-run victory over South Africa
item.description: New Zealand won the Women’s T20 World Cup for the first time, beating South Africa by 32 runs on Sunday after a standout performance from Amelia Kerr with bat and ball.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9
